meals.name: Kefir & Blueberry Smoothie + Boiled Eggs on Sourdough with Feta
meals.image_prompt: overhead photo of a glass of purple blueberry kefir smoothie beside two halved boiled eggs on toasted wholegrain sourdough with crumbled white feta, natural morning light, wooden table, appetising
day_of_steps: 1. Bring a small saucepan of water to the boil. Lower 2 eggs (Diego) or 2 eggs (Diana) gently into the water and boil 9 minutes for hard-boiled. Transfer to cold water, peel, and slice in half.
2. While eggs cook, pour 250 ml kefir into a blender, add the banana and blueberries for each person's smoothie. Blend 30 seconds until smooth. Pour into a glass.
3. Toast 50 g (Diego) / 35 g (Diana) wholegrain sourdough slices.
4. Lay sliced eggs on toast, crumble 30 g (Diego) / 20 g (Diana) feta over the top, season with black pepper.
5. Serve smoothie alongside toast. Drink 500 ml water.

ingredients.item: Wholegrain sourdough bread (pain au levain complet)
Wholegrain sourdough bread (pain au levain complet)

meals.name: Apple & Almond Snack
meals.image_prompt: close-up of a sliced green apple fanned on a small plate next to a small bowl of whole almonds, natural light, minimalist white background
day_of_steps: 1. Rinse and slice 1 apple per person.
2. Weigh 25 g almonds (Diego) / 20 g almonds (Diana) into a small bowl.
3. Serve together. Drink 500 ml water.

meals.name: Mexican Beef & Lentil Stuffed Pepper Bowl with Quinoa
meals.image_prompt: overhead photo of a vibrant bowl with cumin-spiced ground beef and green lentils over golden quinoa, roasted red and yellow bell pepper strips, cherry tomatoes, a drizzle of olive oil, natural light on a terracotta surface
prep_steps: Sunday: Cook quinoa batch — rinse 300 g quinoa, simmer in 600 ml water 15 min, cool and refrigerate.
Sunday: Brown 700 g lean ground beef with garlic, cumin, smoked paprika, chili flakes in a large pan; drain excess fat, mix in drained canned lentils and passata, simmer 10 min. Cool and refrigerate.
Sunday: Roast sliced red and yellow bell peppers and cherry tomatoes at 200°C for 25 min with olive oil, salt, and cumin. Cool and refrigerate.
day_of_steps: 1. Scoop 130 g (Diego) / 90 g (Diana) cooked quinoa into a microwave-safe bowl and reheat 90 seconds, stir.
2. Reheat 220 g (Diego) / 160 g (Diana) beef-lentil mixture in a saucepan over medium heat 3–4 minutes, stirring, until piping hot.
3. Add 100 g (Diego) / 80 g (Diana) roasted peppers and tomatoes alongside the quinoa.
4. Drizzle 2 tsp olive oil over the bowl, squeeze a little bottled lime juice, season with black pepper.
5. Serve immediately. Drink 500 ml water.

ingredients.item: Canned green lentils (lentilles vertes en boîte)
Roasted bell peppers & cherry tomatoes (poivrons et tomates cerises rôtis)
Extra virgin olive oil (huile d'olive extra vierge)
Bottled lime juice (jus de citron vert)
Canned green lentils (lentilles vertes en boîte)
Roasted bell peppers & cherry tomatoes (poivrons et tomates cerises rôtis)
Extra virgin olive oil (huile d'olive extra vierge)
Bottled lime juice (jus de citron vert)

meals.name: Greek Yogurt with Raspberries & Walnuts
meals.image_prompt: close-up of a white ceramic bowl of thick Greek yogurt topped with fresh red raspberries and walnut halves, soft natural afternoon light
day_of_steps: 1. Spoon 180 g (Diego) / 140 g (Diana) plain Greek yogurt (0% or 2%) into a bowl.
2. Add 80 g raspberries and 15 g walnuts (Diego) / 60 g raspberries and 12 g walnuts (Diana) on top.
3. Serve immediately. Drink 500 ml water.

meals.name: Baked Salmon with Roasted Broccoli, Carrots & Sweet Potato Wedges
meals.image_prompt: overhead shot of a baked salmon fillet glazed with soy and ginger beside golden sweet potato wedges, roasted broccoli florets and orange carrot batons on a dark baking tray, warm evening light
day_of_steps: 1. Preheat oven to 200°C.
2. Cut sweet potato (with skin) into wedges: 180 g (Diego) / 120 g (Diana). Toss with 1 tsp olive oil, smoked paprika, sea salt. Spread on one side of a lined baking tray. Bake 10 minutes.
3. Meanwhile, cut broccoli into florets (100 g each) and carrot batons (80 g each). Toss with 1 tsp olive oil, dried thyme, black pepper.
4. After 10 minutes, add vegetables to the other side of the tray.
5. Season salmon fillets (160 g Diego / 120 g Diana) with soy sauce, grated-equivalent ground ginger (½ tsp), and a drizzle of sesame oil. Place on a small oven-proof dish.
6. Bake salmon and vegetables together for a further 14–16 minutes until salmon is cooked through and flakes easily.
7. Plate: vegetables first, then salmon on top, sweet potato wedges alongside. Drink 500 ml water.

ingredients.item: Sweet potato with skin (patate douce avec peau)
Extra virgin olive oil (huile d'olive extra vierge)
Smoked paprika, dried thyme, ground ginger
Sweet potato with skin (patate douce avec peau)
Extra virgin olive oil (huile d'olive extra vierge)
Smoked paprika, dried thyme, ground ginger

meals.name: Kefir & Raspberry Smoothie + Boiled Eggs on Sourdough with Feta
meals.image_prompt: side-view photo of a pink raspberry kefir smoothie glass next to two halved boiled eggs on wholegrain sourdough toast scattered with crumbled feta, bright morning light, light linen placemat
day_of_steps: 1. Boil eggs 9 minutes, transfer to cold water, peel and halve.
2. Blend 250 ml kefir with 100 g raspberries and ½ banana (60 g) per person until smooth.
3. Toast sourdough: 50 g Diego / 35 g Diana.
4. Lay boiled egg halves on toast, crumble feta, season with black pepper.
5. Serve smoothie alongside. Drink 500 ml water.

ingredients.item: Wholegrain sourdough bread (pain au levain complet)
Wholegrain sourdough bread (pain au levain complet)

meals.name: Orange & Walnut Snack
meals.image_prompt: top-down photo of a peeled orange separated into segments on a small plate alongside a handful of walnut halves on white surface, soft natural light
day_of_steps: 1. Peel 1 orange per person and separate into segments.
2. Weigh 20 g walnuts (Diego) / 15 g walnuts (Diana).
3. Serve together. Drink 500 ml water.

meals.name: Asian Soy-Ginger Tilapia Bowl with Stir-Fried Broccoli, Carrots & Brown Rice
meals.image_prompt: overhead bowl photo of soy-ginger glazed tilapia fillet over brown rice with vibrant stir-fried broccoli florets and orange carrot batons, sesame oil drizzle, natural light on a wooden surface
day_of_steps: 1. Cook brown rice: bring water to a boil, add 75 g (Diego) / 55 g (Diana) dry brown rice, simmer 22 minutes. Drain.
2. While rice cooks, mix marinade: 2 tbsp soy sauce, ½ tsp ground ginger, 1 tsp sesame oil, squeeze of lime juice.
3. Marinate tilapia fillets (160 g Diego / 120 g Diana) in the marinade for 5 minutes.
4. Heat 1 tsp olive oil in a non-stick pan over medium-high heat. Cook tilapia 3–4 minutes per side until cooked through. Remove and rest.
5. In the same pan, stir-fry broccoli (100 g) and carrot batons (80 g) per person with 1 tsp sesame oil and a dash of tamari for 4–5 minutes until tender-crisp.
6. Plate: rice first, then vegetables, then tilapia on top. Drizzle remaining marinade from pan. Drink 500 ml water.

ingredients.item: Bottled lime juice (jus de citron vert)
Bottled lime juice (jus de citron vert)

meals.name: Greek Yogurt with Blueberries & Almonds
meals.image_prompt: close-up of thick white Greek yogurt in a small bowl topped with fresh blueberries and sliced almonds, soft afternoon light, marble surface
day_of_steps: 1. Spoon 180 g (Diego) / 140 g (Diana) Greek yogurt into a bowl.
2. Top with 70 g blueberries and 20 g almonds (Diego) / 50 g blueberries and 15 g almonds (Diana).
3. Serve. Drink 500 ml water.

meals.name: Mediterranean Beef & Chickpea Traybake with Roasted Zucchini, Peppers & Sourdough
meals.image_prompt: overhead photo of a rustic oven tray with spiced beef strips and golden chickpeas among roasted zucchini rounds and colourful bell pepper pieces, a slice of sourdough on the side, warm Mediterranean light
prep_steps: Sunday: Roast chickpeas + zucchini + peppers with oregano, olive oil, salt for 25 min at 200°C. Refrigerate.
day_of_steps: 1. Reheat Sunday's roasted chickpea-vegetable mix (120 g Diego / 90 g Diana) in a pan over medium heat for 4–5 minutes.
2. In a separate pan, cook 150 g (Diego) / 110 g (Diana) thin lean beef strips with 1 tsp olive oil, dried oregano, smoked paprika, black pepper, 2–3 minutes per side until cooked through.
3. Combine beef with reheated vegetables in the pan, add a splash of red wine vinegar, toss.
4. Toast 50 g (Diego) / 35 g (Diana) sourdough.
5. Plate vegetables and beef; serve with sourdough on the side. Drink 500 ml water.

Canned chickpeas, drained (pois chiches en boîte égouttés)
Extra virgin olive oil (huile d'olive extra vierge)
Wholegrain sourdough bread (pain au levain complet)
Red wine vinegar (vinaigre de vin rouge)
Dried oregano, smoked paprika, black pepper

day_of_steps: 1. Boil eggs 9 minutes, cool in cold water, peel and halve.
2. Blend 250 ml kefir with 80 g blueberries and 80 g banana per person. Blend smooth.
3. Toast sourdough: 50 g Diego / 35 g Diana.
4. Place egg halves on toast, crumble feta (30 g Diego / 20 g Diana), black pepper.
5. Serve smoothie alongside toast. Drink 500 ml water.

meals.name: Apple & Pistachio Snack
meals.image_prompt: flat lay of a crisp apple sliced into wedges alongside a small pile of shelled pistachios on a light grey surface, fresh natural light

2. Weigh 25 g pistachios (Diego) / 20 g pistachios (Diana).
3. Serve. Drink 500 ml water.

meals.name: Mexican Beef & Black Bean Corn Tortilla Bowls with Roasted Peppers & Spinach
meals.image_prompt: overhead photo of two warm corn tortillas filled with spiced beef strips, black lentils, wilted spinach, roasted yellow and red peppers, drizzled with lime juice and olive oil, fresh natural light on terracotta
prep_steps: Sunday: Roast bell pepper strips with olive oil, cumin, salt at 200°C 25 min. Refrigerate.
Sunday: Season and cook lean beef (ground or strips) with garlic, cumin, chili flakes. Cool, refrigerate.
day_of_steps: 1. Warm corn tortillas (2 for Diego / 2 for Diana) in a dry pan 30 seconds each side.
2. Reheat 150 g (Diego) / 110 g (Diana) cooked beef in pan over medium heat 3–4 min. Add drained canned lentils (60 g Diego / 45 g Diana) and stir to warm through.
3. In the same pan, wilt 60 g fresh spinach per person with 1 tsp olive oil and a pinch of cumin, 2 minutes.
4. Reheat roasted peppers (80 g per person) briefly in pan.
5. Fill each tortilla: spinach first, then beef-lentil mix, then peppers. Squeeze lime juice over, drizzle 1 tsp olive oil.
6. Serve with 100 g mixed leaf salad greens tossed with balsamic vinegar. Drink 500 ml water.

ingredients.item: Canned green lentils (lentilles vertes en boîte)
Extra virgin olive oil (huile d'olive extra vierge)
Canned green lentils (lentilles vertes en boîte)
Extra virgin olive oil (huile d'olive extra vierge)

meals.image_prompt: overhead close-up of Greek yogurt in a small bowl with scattered raspberries and walnut pieces, soft afternoon window light
day_of_steps: 1. Spoon 180 g (Diego) / 140 g (Diana) Greek yogurt into a bowl.

meals.name: French-Style Salmon en Papillote with Zucchini, Carrots & Herbed Potatoes
meals.image_prompt: overhead photo of a just-opened parchment parcel revealing a perfectly cooked salmon fillet with zucchini ribbons and carrot coins, golden herb-seasoned potato slices alongside on a white plate, soft natural light
day_of_steps: 1. Preheat oven to 200°C.
2. Slice waxy potatoes (skin on, 160 g Diego / 110 g Diana) into thin rounds. Toss with 1 tsp olive oil, dried thyme, sea salt, black pepper. Spread on a lined tray; bake 25 minutes.
3. Cut 2 large squares of baking parchment. Place 60 g zucchini rounds and 60 g carrot coins (per person) in centre. Season with dried basil, black pepper, drizzle of olive oil.
4. Lay salmon fillet (160 g Diego / 120 g Diana) on top of vegetables. Add 1 tsp olive oil, squeeze of lime juice, pinch of dried oregano.
5. Fold parchment into tight parcels. Bake 18–20 minutes until salmon is opaque.
6. Open parcels carefully (steam). Serve salmon parcels alongside potato slices. Drink 500 ml water.

ingredients.item: Waxy potatoes with skin (pommes de terre à chair ferme avec peau)
Extra virgin olive oil (huile d'olive extra vierge)
Dried thyme, dried basil, dried oregano, black pepper

3. Toast sourdough: 50 g Diego / 35 g Diana.
4. Top toast with egg halves and crumbled feta (30 g Diego / 20 g Diana), black pepper.
5. Serve smoothie alongside. Drink 500 ml water.

meals.name: Creamy Tomato Chicken Thighs with Wilted Spinach & Wholemeal Penne
meals.image_prompt: overhead photo of penne pasta tossed with creamy tomato sauce, tender chicken thigh pieces and wilted spinach in a wide bowl, garnished with dried basil, natural light on a white surface
day_of_steps: 1. Cook wholemeal penne: boil salted water, add 85 g (Diego) / 65 g (Diana) dry pasta, cook 11 minutes. Drain, reserve 50 ml pasta water.
2. Season 170 g (Diego) / 125 g (Diana) skinless chicken thighs with smoked paprika, dried oregano, salt, pepper.
3. Heat 1 tsp olive oil in a wide pan, sear chicken thighs 5–6 min per side until cooked through (internal 75°C). Remove and slice.

4. In same pan, add 150 g (both Diego and Diana) tomato passata, garlic (from locked list — use ½ tsp garlic powder or a minced clove from pantry), chili flakes, dried basil. Simmer 3 minutes. Stir in 2 tbsp Greek yogurt per person for creaminess.
5. Wilt 70 g fresh spinach per person in sauce for 1–2 minutes.
6. Toss drained pasta into sauce with a splash of pasta water. Top with sliced chicken.
7. Plate pasta first, then sauce, then chicken. Drink 500 ml water.

ingredients.item: Skinless chicken thighs (cuisses de poulet sans peau)
Extra virgin olive oil (huile d'olive extra vierge)
Smoked paprika, dried oregano, dried basil, chili flakes
Skinless chicken thighs (cuisses de poulet sans peau)
Extra virgin olive oil (huile d'olive extra vierge)
Smoked paprika, dried oregano, dried basil, chili flakes

day_of_steps: 1. Spoon 180 g (Diego) / 140 g (Diana) Greek yogurt into a bowl.

3. Serve. Drink 500 ml water.

meals.name: Baked Tilapia with Roasted Broccoli, Cherry Tomatoes & Cannellini Beans on Sourdough
meals.image_prompt: overhead dinner plate with oven-baked tilapia fillet resting on creamy cannellini beans and roasted broccoli florets and burst cherry tomatoes, a slice of crusty sourdough on the side, warm evening light
day_of_steps: 1. Preheat oven to 200°C.
2. Toss broccoli florets (90 g per person) and cherry tomatoes (80 g per person) with 1 tsp olive oil, dried oregano, sea salt, black pepper. Spread on lined tray. Roast 20 minutes.
3. Season tilapia fillets (150 g Diego / 115 g Diana) with smoked paprika, dried thyme, black pepper, 1 tsp olive oil. Place on separate small tray.
4. Bake tilapia for last 14–16 minutes alongside vegetables until cooked through.
5. Warm drained canned cannellini beans (70 g Diego / 50 g Diana) in a small pan with 1 tsp olive oil, garlic powder, red wine vinegar, 2 minutes.
6. Toast sourdough: 50 g Diego / 35 g Diana.
7. Plate: beans first, then vegetables and tilapia, sourdough on side. Drink 500 ml water.

2. Weigh 25 g cashews (Diego) / 20 g cashews (Diana).

meals.name: Asian Sesame Beef Stir-Fry with Zucchini, Broccoli & Wholewheat Noodles
meals.image_prompt: overhead bowl of wholewheat noodles tossed with dark soy-sesame sauce, thin beef strips, bright green broccoli florets and zucchini ribbons, sesame oil gloss, natural light on bamboo mat
day_of_steps: 1. Cook wholewheat noodles (80 g Diego / 60 g Diana dry) in boiling salted water per package (8–10 min). Drain and toss with 1 tsp sesame oil.
2. Slice lean beef (160 g Diego / 120 g Diana) into thin strips. Marinate 5 min in 2 tbsp soy sauce, ½ tsp ground ginger, ½ tsp cinnamon (optional), black pepper.
3. Heat 1 tsp sesame oil in wok or large pan, high heat. Stir-fry beef 3 min until browned. Remove.
4. Stir-fry broccoli (90 g) and zucchini (90 g) per person in same pan with 1 tsp soy sauce, 2 min.
5. Return beef to pan, add drained canned chickpeas (50 g per person), toss all together 1–2 min.
6. Serve over noodles, drizzle with balsamic vinegar (¼ tsp) for depth. Drink 500 ml water.

ingredients.item: Lean beef strips (lanières de bœuf maigre)
Canned chickpeas, drained (pois chiches en boîte égouttés)
Lean beef strips (lanières de bœuf maigre)

3. Serve. Drink 500 ml water.

meals.name: Mediterranean Baked Salmon with Chickpea-Spinach Ragù & Roasted Bell Peppers
meals.image_prompt: overhead photo of a baked salmon fillet on a bed of golden chickpeas and wilted spinach ragù surrounded by roasted red and yellow bell pepper strips, drizzled with olive oil, warm restaurant lighting
day_of_steps: 1. Preheat oven to 200°C. Slice bell peppers. Toss with 1 tsp olive oil, dried oregano, sea salt. Roast 20 minutes.
2. Season salmon (160 g Diego / 120 g Diana) with smoked paprika, dried basil, black pepper, 1 tsp olive oil. Place on a small oven-proof dish. Bake 16–18 minutes.
3. In a pan, heat 1 tsp olive oil, add drained chickpeas (80 g Diego / 60 g Diana), passata (80 g per person), ground coriander, chili flakes. Simmer 5 min.
4. Stir in 70 g fresh spinach per person; wilt 2 minutes. Season with red wine vinegar (1 tsp).
5. Plate: chickpea-spinach ragù first, salmon on top, roasted peppers alongside. Drink 500 ml water.

4. Arrange egg halves on toast, crumble feta (30 g Diego / 20 g Diana), black pepper.

meals.name: French Lentil & Roasted Vegetable Salad with Poached Egg & Sourdough
meals.image_prompt: overhead salad bowl with warm green lentils, roasted cherry tomatoes, zucchini and bell pepper strips over mixed greens, topped with a perfectly poached egg and a balsamic drizzle, rustic wooden surface
day_of_steps: 1. Preheat oven to 200°C. Toss cherry tomatoes (80 g), zucchini rounds (80 g), and bell pepper strips (80 g) per person with 1 tsp olive oil, dried thyme, sea salt. Roast 20 minutes.
2. Warm drained canned lentils (100 g Diego / 75 g Diana) in a pan with 1 tsp olive oil, ground coriander, balsamic vinegar (1 tsp), salt, pepper.
3. Bring a small saucepan of water to a bare simmer, add 1 tsp red wine vinegar. Crack each egg into a cup and slide gently into water. Poach 3–4 minutes until white is set.
4. Toast sourdough: 50 g Diego / 35 g Diana.
5. Plate: mixed salad greens (80 g), then warm lentils, then roasted vegetables. Place poached egg on top. Drizzle 1 tsp olive oil. Serve with sourdough. Drink 500 ml water.

meals.name: Honey-Glazed Tilapia with Stir-Fried Celery, Zucchini & Sweet Potato Mash
meals.image_prompt: overhead plate of golden honey-glazed tilapia fillet beside creamy sweet potato mash and stir-fried celery and zucchini, warm evening light, natural rustic wooden table
day_of_steps: 1. Peel and cube sweet potato (180 g Diego / 120 g Diana). Boil in salted water 15 minutes until tender. Drain, mash with 1 tsp olive oil, black pepper, pinch of cinnamon.
2. Mix glaze: 1 tsp soy sauce + ½ tsp sesame oil + squeeze of lime juice. Brush onto tilapia fillets (160 g Diego / 120 g Diana).
3. Heat 1 tsp olive oil in non-stick pan. Cook tilapia 3–4 min per side until caramelised and cooked through.
4. In same pan, stir-fry celery (2 stalks, 80 g, sliced) and zucchini (90 g, rounds) per person with 1 tsp sesame oil, tamari, dried basil, 4–5 minutes.
5. Plate: sweet potato mash, stir-fried vegetables, tilapia on top. Drink 500 ml water.

meals.image_prompt: overhead of a pink-purple smoothie glass with banana and raspberries beside thick sourdough toast topped with halved boiled eggs and white feta crumbles, relaxed Sunday morning light
day_of_steps: 1. Boil eggs 9 minutes, cool, peel, halve.
2. Blend 250 ml kefir with 80 g raspberries and 80 g banana per person.
3. Toast sourdough: 50 g Diego / 35 g Diana.
4. Top with egg halves, crumble feta (30 g Diego / 20 g Diana), season with black pepper.
5. Serve smoothie. Drink 500 ml water.

ingredients.item: Wholegrain sourdough bread (pain au levain complet)
Wholegrain sourdough bread (pain au levain complet)

meals.name: Apple & Walnut Snack
meals.image_prompt: overhead of apple slices and walnut halves on a small wooden board, warm weekend morning light
day_of_steps: 1. Slice 1 apple per person.
2. Weigh 20 g walnuts (Diego) / 15 g walnuts (Diana).
3. Serve. Drink 500 ml water.

meals.name: Beef & Roasted Vegetable Quinoa Salad with Balsamic Dressing
meals.image_prompt: overhead of a large salad bowl with golden quinoa, thin seared beef strips, roasted cherry tomatoes, bell pepper chunks and cucumber slices, dressed with balsamic vinegar and olive oil, bright Sunday daylight
day_of_steps: 1. Cook quinoa: rinse 70 g (Diego) / 50 g (Diana) dry quinoa, simmer in water 15 min, cool slightly.
2. Preheat oven 200°C. Toss cherry tomatoes (80 g) and bell pepper strips (80 g) per person with 1 tsp olive oil, dried oregano. Roast 20 min.
3. Season lean beef strips (160 g Diego / 120 g Diana) with smoked paprika, cumin, black pepper. Sear in hot pan with 1 tsp olive oil, 2–3 min each side. Rest and slice.
4. Slice cucumber (60 g per person) and add drained chickpeas (60 g per person).
5. Make dressing: 2 tsp olive oil + 1 tsp balsamic vinegar + pinch dried oregano per person.
6. Assemble: quinoa base, then roasted vegetables, cucumber, chickpeas, beef on top. Drizzle dressing. Drink 500 ml water.

ingredients.item: Lean beef strips (lanières de bœuf maigre)
Canned chickpeas, drained (pois chiches en boîte égouttés)
Extra virgin olive oil (huile d'olive extra vierge)
Lean beef strips (lanières de bœuf maigre)

meals.name: Greek Yogurt with Raspberries & Pistachios
meals.image_prompt: close-up of a white Greek yogurt bowl with scattered red raspberries and bright green pistachios, golden Sunday afternoon light
day_of_steps: 1. Spoon 180 g (Diego) / 140 g (Diana) Greek yogurt into a bowl.
2. Top with 70 g raspberries and 15 g pistachios (Diego) / 50 g raspberries and 12 g pistachios (Diana).
3. Serve. Drink 500 ml water.

meals.name: Tomato-Oregano Baked Tilapia with Cannellini Beans, Wilted Spinach & Sourdough
meals.image_prompt: overhead of a baked tilapia fillet in a tomato-oregano sauce surrounded by creamy white cannellini beans and wilted spinach in a white baking dish, sourdough slice on the side, warm Sunday evening light
day_of_steps: 1. Preheat oven to 190°C.
2. Spread passata (100 g per person) in the base of an oven-proof dish. Add dried oregano, chili flakes, black pepper.
3. Rinse and add drained cannellini beans (70 g Diego / 50 g Diana) to sauce, stir.
4. Lay tilapia fillets (150 g Diego / 115 g Diana) on top of beans. Drizzle 1 tsp olive oil each, dust with smoked paprika.
5. Bake 18–20 minutes until fish flakes and sauce bubbles.
6. Stir in 70 g fresh spinach per person, return to oven for 2 minutes until wilted.
7. Toast sourdough: 50 g Diego / 35 g Diana. Serve alongside baking dish. Drink 500 ml water.

Extra virgin olive oil (huile d'olive extra vierge)
